content:
# I Built an Autonomous Vehicle Analytics Platform (Solo) вҖ” Here's What I Learned

And why I'm open-sourcing it all.

The Origin Story

It started simple: What if one person could build a real-time analytics platform for autonomous vehicles?

Not the software for driving them. But something that watches them, learns from them, and makes sense of all that sensor data streaming in. Something that could scale from tracking one vehicle to fifty.

Three months ago, I decided to find out.

Today, I'm launching VehicleMetrics вҖ” the full codebase, architecture diagrams, deployment guides, and everything вҖ” on GitHub. And I want to tell you why this matters, even if you don't care about autonomous vehicles.

Because the lesson isn't about AVs. It's about building production systems solo.

The Challenge

Here's what I wanted to prove:

вҖў
One developer (me)

вҖў
One vehicle (to start with, or simulated data)

вҖў
One month for a proof-of-concept

вҖў
Production-ready architecture that could scale to 50+ vehicles

No shortcuts. No "we'll refactor later." Real infrastructure, real security, real DevOps.

The catch? I had to use AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, PostgreSQL, Redis, React, FastAPI, and a CI/CD pipeline. All while working solo.

Most people would say that's insane.

I said, "let's build."

What We Built

VehicleMetrics is a 3-tier cloud-native system designed to ingest, process, and analyze vehicle sensor data in real-time.

The Architecture (The Part Everyone Asks About)

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ PRESENTATION TIER в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ React + TypeScript + Grafana Dashboards в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ (Real-time Analytics UI) в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
в”Ӯ HTTPS / WebSocket
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ APPLICATION TIER в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ FastAPI Microservices on EKS (Kubernetes) в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў Ingestion Service вҖў Analytics Service в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў Auth Service вҖў Aggregation Service в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
в”Ӯ VPC / Private Subnets
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ DATA TIER в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB (Time-Series Data) в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў Redis (Caching & Real-Time Streams) в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў S3 (Data Lake for Raw Sensor Data) в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҖў Kinesis (Real-Time Data Pipeline) в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Enter fullscreen mode

Exit fullscreen mode

It's the kind of architecture you'd build at a startup with 5 engineers. Except one person built it.

Why? Because boring architecture wins. Nothing custom. Nothing clever. All battle-tested, industry-standard tools that have proven themselves a million times over.

The Tech Stack (No Surprises Here)

Layer
Tech
Why

Compute
AWS EKS (Kubernetes)
Industry standard for container orchestration

Infrastructure
Terraform
IAC done right. Version control everything.

Backend
FastAPI + Python 3.11
Fast, modern, great for microservices

Frontend
React + TypeScript
Type safety. Component-driven. Community support.

Database
PostgreSQL 14 + TimescaleDB
ACID compliance + time-series specialization

Cache
Redis 7
Blazingly fast. Proven. Boring.

Data Pipeline
AWS Kinesis + Lambda
Stream processing at scale

Monitoring
Prometheus + Grafana
Observability you can understand

CI/CD
GitHub Actions
Right here where the code lives

Every choice was made for the same reason: When things break at 2 AM, I need to fix them alone. So I chose tools I could understand, debug, and reason about.

The Timeline (How I Actually Did This)

Phase 1: Proof of Concept (Month 1)

вҖў Week 1: Architecture & AWS Setup

вҖў Week 2: Infrastructure (EKS, RDS, S3 configured)

вҖў Week 3: Core API (FastAPI ingestion service)

вҖў Week 4: Dashboard (React + real data visualization)

Phase 2: Production (Months 2-6)

вҖў Multi-vehicle support

вҖў Advanced analytics

вҖў Alert system

вҖў Performance optimization

Phase 3: Intelligence (Months 7-12)

вҖў AI/ML integration (GPT-4, Claude, Llama)

вҖў Predictive analytics

вҖў Anomaly detection

вҖў Custom model training

What Shocked Me

1. The 80/20 Rule is Real

80% of the work was the infrastructure and plumbing. The actual "vehicle analytics" code? Maybe 20%.

This means: invest in architecture first. The business logic is easy. Getting it to scale is the hard part.

2. Documentation > Code

I spent as much time writing deployment guides as I did writing code. And you know what? That's the right ratio.

Future me (and future maintainers) will thank me.

3. You Don't Need a Team

You need:

вҖў Clear constraints (1 month, 1 vehicle)

вҖў Battle-tested tools (no experiments)

вҖў Ruthless scope management

вҖў Good documentation

вҖў Boring choices

You DON'T need:

вҖў Meetings

вҖў Design reviews

вҖў Consensus

вҖў Enterprise process

Solo developers move fast.

Why Open Source?

Three reasons:

вҖў Teach вҖ” This is a real, production-ready system. Use it to learn how real architectures work.

вҖў Prove вҖ” Solo developers can build enterprise systems. You don't need a team for this.

вҖў Build вҖ” Take this, fork it, modify it, scale it. Make something better. I genuinely want to see what you build with this.

What's Included

Everything is on GitHub at beltagyy/vehicle-metrics:

вң… Complete source code (backend, frontend, infrastructure)

вң… Terraform templates for full AWS deployment

вң… Kubernetes manifests (development and production)

вң… Docker Compose for local development

вң… API reference (complete endpoint documentation)

вң… Security guide (including ISO 21434 compliance notes)

вң… Operations manual (what to do when things break)

вң… 17 Phase 1 GitHub issues (ready to start working)

вң… MIT License (do what you want with it)

Clone it. Deploy it. Learn from it. Improve it.

The Real Lesson

This project isn't really about autonomous vehicles.

It's about proving that good engineering beats team size.

One person with:

вҖў Clear goals

вҖў Boring tools

вҖў Good documentation

вҖў Real constraints

...can build something that actually scales.

Most teams fail because they optimize for process instead of output. I succeeded because I optimized for speed and clarity.

What's Next?

вҖў
For me: Building Phase 2 (multi-vehicle support) and adding AI/ML capabilities

вҖў
For you: Check out the repo, read the ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy it locally with Docker Compose

Questions? Issues? Improvements?

Hit me up on GitHub (@beltagyy) or in the issues. I built this to be learned from, modified, and improved.

TL;DR

вҖў Built a production-grade autonomous vehicle analytics platform solo

вҖў Used AWS, Kubernetes, FastAPI, React, PostgreSQL, Redis, and good engineering practices

вҖў Open-sourced everything at github.com/beltagyy/vehicle-metrics

вҖў Proof that solo developers can build enterprise systems with boring, proven tools

вҖў Phase 1 done. Phase 2 coming soon.

Come build with me. рҹҡҖ

GitHub: beltagyy/vehicle-metrics

Author: Mohamed ElBeltagy (@beltagyy)
